walt_content: Discuss free time routines and active hobbies fluently. Shift away from Band 5.0 hesitation by using adverbs of frequency to describe habits naturally.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Provide sentence frames on the board if weaker students freeze during the speaking circuit: "My favorite pastime is ____ because..." and "I usually participate in ____." Allow them to rely on their Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Demand exact execution of the complex sentence without reading. Direct fast-finishers to the Part 3 Differentiation Stretch question (Section 10) to force spontaneous, unscripted reasoning.
grammar_content:
• Adverbs: Place them before the main verb but after the 'be' verb (e.g., I usually play... / I am always happy...).
副词：放在实义动词之前，但在be动词之后 (例如: I usually play... / I am always happy...)

• Gerunds: Verbs expressing preference (enjoy, prefer, love) are followed by a V-ing gerund (e.g., I enjoy playing badminton...).
动名词：表达喜好的动词 (enjoy, prefer, love) 后面接 V-ing 形式 (例如: I enjoy playing badminton...)
pronunciation_content:
To sound like a native speaker, do not pause between words if one ends in a consonant sound and the next begins with a vowel sound. Slide them together!
为了听起来像母语者，如果一个单词以辅音结尾，下一个单词以元音开头，请不要停顿。把它们连起来读！

• Say: have‿a blast (Sounds like: ha - va - blast)

• Say: couch‿potato (Smoothly connect the ch into the p)
idioms: [{"phrase": "Couch potato", "meaning": "\u7535\u89c6\u8ff7 / \u61d2\u9aa8\u5934", "explanation": "\u6307\u559c\u6b22\u6574\u5929\u5750\u5728\u6c99\u53d1\u4e0a\u770b\u7535\u89c6\u3001\u4e0d\u7231\u8fd0\u52a8\u7684\u4eba\u3002", "example": "On weekends, I become a couch potato and watch TV shows all day.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Have a blast", "meaning": "\u5c3d\u60c5\u6b22\u4e50 / \u73a9\u5f97\u75db\u5feb", "explanation": "\u73a9\u5f97\u975e\u5e38\u5f00\u5fc3\uff0c\u5ea6\u8fc7\u4e86\u4e00\u6bb5\u6781\u597d\u7684\u65f6\u5149\u3002", "example": "We went to the music festival last night and had a blast.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What activities did you usually do to relax after a long day at middle school?", "Do you prefer spending your free time alone or hanging out with friends?", "Have your hobbies changed since you started Grade 10?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, finding free time is hard for Grade 10 students. Nowadays, our schedules are very busy with heavy homework loads. However, it is still very important to unwind. Usually, many of my classmates act like a couch potato on weekends. They just watch videos online and sleep all day. Personally, I prefer active recreation. For example, I am a huge basketball enthusiast, so I always participate in school matches. When I play sports with my friends, we always have a blast. Overall, staying active is a truly fascinating pastime.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a leisure activity you enjoy doing regularly. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What it is and what equipment is needed.", "Who you do it with.", "Explain why this is your ideal way to relax."]
model_answer: Well, my favorite pastime is playing badminton. Normally, I play it every Saturday afternoon at the local sports center. Actually, I rarely go alone, so I always participate with my best friends. To be honest, I used to be a couch potato before high school, but now, I am a huge sports enthusiast. Because high school is very stressful, I always get some exercise to unwind. In fact, running around the court is a fascinating form of recreation. To sum up, we highly enjoy this activity, and we always have a blast.

walt_content: Discuss memorable TV shows and movies using comparatives and superlatives. Evaluate content effectively using target vocabulary.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Provide sentence frames: "My favorite show is ____ because it is more..." and "I think documentaries are the most..." Allow them to rely on their Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Demand exact execution of the complex sentence without reading. Direct fast-finishers to the Part 3 Differentiation Stretch question (Section 10) to force spontaneous comparing and evaluating.
grammar_content:
• Comparatives: Compare two things (e.g., News is more informative than cartoons. / This show is funnier than that one.).
比较级：比较两件事物 (例如: News is more informative than cartoons. / This show is funnier than that one.)

• Superlatives: Compare three or more things (e.g., It is the most entertaining show on TV. / He is the best actor.).
最高级：比较三件或更多事物 (例如: It is the most entertaining show on TV. / He is the best actor.)
pronunciation_content:
English words have one stressed syllable that is longer, louder, and higher in pitch. If you stress the wrong syllable, it can be hard to understand.
英语单词有一个重读音节，发音更长、更响亮、音调更高。如果你重读错误的音节，可能会很难理解。

• Say: In-FOR-ma-tive (Stress the 2nd syllable)

• Say: En-ter-TAIN-ing (Stress the 3rd syllable)

• Say: Ad-DIC-tive (Stress the 2nd syllable)
vocab_rows: [{"word": "Broadcast", "type": "V/N", "meaning": "广播 / 播出"}, {"word": "Commercial", "type": "N/Adj", "meaning": "商业广告 / 商业的"}, {"word": "Informative", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "提供有用信息的"}, {"word": "Entertaining", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "娱乐的 / 有趣的"}, {"word": "Subscribe", "type": "V", "meaning": "订阅 / 关注"}, {"word": "Addictive", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "使人上瘾的"}, {"word": "Unwind", "type": "V", "meaning": "放松 / 解压"}, {"word": "Fascinating", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "迷人的 / 极好的"}]
idioms: [{"phrase": "Glued to the screen", "meaning": "目不转睛 / 紧盯屏幕", "explanation": "形容人全神贯注地盯着电视或手机屏幕看。", "example": "My little brother is always glued to the screen when his favorite cartoon is on.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Make headlines", "meaning": "登上头条 / 轰动一时", "explanation": "成为新闻的重要报道，引起广泛关注。", "example": "The release of the new smartphone made headlines yesterday.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What show on TV do you find most fascinating and why?", "Do you usually participate in any sports to unwind after school?", "Are you more of a couch potato or an active enthusiast on weekends?"]
transcoded_input: Honestly speaking, I spend too much time on my phone. These days, short videos are very addictive. I subscribe to many funny channels because they are entertaining. However, my parents think I should watch the news broadcast. They say it is more informative than cartoons. Sometimes, I agree with them. Actually, watching a good documentary is a great way to unwind. Although the commercials are annoying, the show itself is fascinating. In conclusion, I try not to be glued to the screen all day.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a memorable movie or TV show. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What it is and what happens in it.", "Who your favorite character is.", "Explain why you think it is worth watching."]
model_answer: Well, the most memorable show for me is 'Planet Earth'. Basically, it is a nature documentary that is broadcast on the BBC. To be honest, it is much more fascinating than a normal movie. I subscribe to their channel so I never miss an episode. The images are so beautiful that I am always glued to the screen. In fact, it often makes headlines around the world. Even though it is educational, it is also highly entertaining and addictive. All in all, it is the most informative show I have ever seen.
listener_task_content:
Peer Assessment: Tally how many Blue starters your partner uses.

Action: Give them a "thumbs-up" the exact moment you hear them use their Yellow complex sentence!

Support: If your partner freezes, help them with these starters:
"My favorite show is ____ because..."
"I think documentaries are..."
fast_finisher_question:
If your partner finishes speaking before the timer runs out, ask them this Part 3 Question:


"Do you think young people today watch too much TV compared to the past? Why?"
exit_ticket_content:
Fix the Error (Comparative Adjectives): Write the correct form of the adjective.



1. This movie is ___________ (interesting) than that one.

2. News is ___________ (boring) than sports.

3. He is the ___________ (funny) actor in the world.

walt_content: Describe memorable meals and eating habits using specific vocabulary. Use the Present Perfect Tense to talk about life experiences with food.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "My favorite meal is ____ because..." and "I have never eaten ____." rely on Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Use sensory adjectives (spicy, bitter, crunchy) to describe food in detail. Direct fast-finishers to Section 10.
grammar_content:
• Quantifiers: Use too much (uncountable) and too many (countable). (e.g., I ate too much cake / too many cookies).
量词：too much + 不可数名词; too many + 可数名词

• Present Perfect: Use have/has + V3 for experiences. (e.g., I have tried sushi before.).
现在完成时：have/has + 过去分词，表示经历
pronunciation_content:
Stretch out the vowel sounds in words like food (fuuuud), eat (eeet), and meal (meeeal). Do not cut them short!
长元音：拉长元音发音，不要读得太短促！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Have a sweet tooth", "meaning": "\u55dc\u751c\u5982\u547d / \u559c\u98df\u751c\u98df", "explanation": "\u6781\u5176\u559c\u6b22\u5403\u7cd6\u679c\u3001\u751c\u70b9\u7b49\u751c\u98df\u3002", "example": "I have a sweet tooth, so I always order chocolate cake after dinner.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "A piece of cake", "meaning": "\u5c0f\u83dc\u4e00\u789f / \u6613\u5982\u53cd\u638c", "explanation": "\u5f62\u5bb9\u505a\u67d0\u4ef6\u4e8b\u60c5\u975e\u5e38\u5bb9\u6613\uff0c\u6beb\u4e0d\u8d39\u529b\u3002", "example": "For a professional chef, cooking a big family dinner is a piece of cake.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What is the most entertaining cooking show you have ever watched? (Recycled vocab)", "Do you prefer homemade food or eating out at restaurants?", "Have you ever tried to cook a nutritious meal by yourself?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, I have always had a huge appetite. Recently, I watched an informative documentary about sugar, and I learned that it is very addictive. However, I have a sweet tooth, so it is hard for me to stop eating cakes. Usually, I try to consume healthy ingredients like vegetables and fish. Although fast food is tasty, I know that homemade food is much more nutritious. Actually, I have tried Japanese cuisine many times, and I love it. Overall, cooking healthy food is not a piece of cake, but it is worth it.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a memorable meal you have had. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you had it and who you were with.", "What specific dishes and ingredients you ate.", "Explain why this meal was so memorable for you."]
model_answer: Well, the most memorable meal I have ever had was a dinner at my grandmother's house. Normally, we visit her every Spring Festival. Actually, she is an amazing cook, and her homemade dumplings are delicious. To be honest, the ingredients she uses are always fresh from her garden. Because the food was so nutritious and tasty, I consumed three huge bowls! In fact, I have such a big appetite when I am there. To sum up, traditional Chinese cuisine is my favorite, and eating with family is never a piece of cake to forget. Also, learning about her recipes was very informative and the taste was truly addictive.

exit_ticket_content:
Fix the Error (Countable vs Uncountable): Circle the correct word.

1. I ate too ( much / many ) rice.

2. There are too ( much / many ) apples on the table.

3. She drinks too ( much / many ) water.

walt_content: Discuss journeys and transport modes. Use the First Conditional to talk about future travel possibilities.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I usually travel by..." and "If I go to..., I will..."
stretch_content: Use adverbs of probability (definitely, probably) with the First Conditional.
grammar_content:
• First Conditional: If + Present Simple, ... will + Verb. (e.g., If it rains, I will take the bus.).
第一条件句：If + 一般现在时, ... will + 动词原形 (例如: If it rains, I will take the bus.)
pronunciation_content:
Stress the key words (verbs and nouns) in both parts of the sentence. Emphasize the result!
条件句中的重音：重读句子两部分中的关键词 (动词和名词)。强调结果！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Beat the traffic", "meaning": "\u9519\u5cf0\u51fa\u884c", "explanation": "\u63d0\u65e9\u51fa\u53d1\u4ee5\u907f\u5f00\u9053\u8def\u4ea4\u901a\u9ad8\u5cf0\u671f\u3002", "example": "We need to leave the house at 6 AM to beat the traffic.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Miss the boat", "meaning": "\u9519\u5931\u826f\u673a / \u5750\u5931\u673a\u5b9c", "explanation": "\u56e0\u4e3a\u884c\u52a8\u592a\u6162\u800c\u9519\u5931\u4e86\u826f\u673a\u3002", "example": "I forgot to buy the cheap train tickets yesterday, so I missed the boat.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) How do you usually commute to school every day?", "If you consume too much junk food before a long trip, how do you feel? (Recycled)", "Is the public transport in your city usually reliable?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, my daily commute to school is quite long. Usually, I take the subway because it is very convenient. However, during rush hour, there are too many passengers. If I wake up late tomorrow, I will definitely miss the bus and be late for class. Sometimes, my father drives me in his vehicle, but the congestion is terrible. Actually, we always try to leave early to beat the traffic. In my opinion, taking the subway is more reliable than driving. Also, it's safer because I don't consume fuel or pollute the air, which is not very nutritious for the planet.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a long journey you have taken. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you went and how you traveled.", "What happened during the journey.", "Explain how you felt about the trip."]
model_answer: Well, last summer I went to Beijing by train. Actually, it was a very long journey, taking over five hours. To be honest, the train was very comfortable and spacious for every passenger. However, we almost missed the boat because we arrived at the station late! If I visit Beijing again, I will definitely take the high-speed train to save time. Fortunately, the train was reliable and we arrived on time. Overall, despite the congestion near the station, it was a convenient way to travel. I also managed to consume a very nutritious meal on board.

walt_content: Describe unforgettable trips and travel experiences. Use Past Continuous vs Past Simple to tell stories.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I went to ____." and "While I was walking, I saw..."
stretch_content: Use narrative tenses to create suspense or interest in your story.
grammar_content:
• Past Continuous: Was/Were + V-ing (Background action).
• Past Simple: V-ed (Interrupted action).
• While I was sleeping (continuous), the phone rang (simple).
过去进行时 vs 一般过去时：正在进行的背景动作被打断 (例如: While I was sleeping, the phone rang.)
pronunciation_content:
In fast speech, 'was' sounds like /wəz/ and 'were' sounds like /wə/. Don't stress them!
弱读：在快速语流中，was 读作 /wəz/，were 读作 /wə/。不要重读它们！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Travel light", "meaning": "\u8f7b\u88c5\u7b80\u884c / \u8f7b\u88c5\u4e0a\u9635", "explanation": "\u51fa\u95e8\u65c5\u884c\u65f6\u5c3d\u91cf\u5c11\u5e26\u884c\u674e\u3002", "example": "I always travel light because I hate carrying heavy bags at the airport.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Off the beaten track", "meaning": "\u4eba\u8ff9\u7f55\u81f3 / \u4e16\u5916\u6843\u6e90", "explanation": "\u53bb\u504f\u50fb\u7684\u3001\u5f88\u5c11\u6709\u6e38\u5ba2\u53bb\u7684\u51b7\u95e8\u5730\u65b9\u3002", "example": "We found a beautiful small beach that was completely off the beaten track.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What is your dream holiday destination?", "Is public transport convenient in your city for tourists? (Recycled)", "Do you prefer to plan a strict itinerary or just relax?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, I love traveling. Last year, I went to Yunnan, which is a popular destination. While I was looking for accommodation online, I found a small hotel off the beaten track. However, the traffic congestion on the way there was bad. While I was hiking up the mountain, I saw the most breathtaking view of the clouds. Actually, I prefer to travel light so I can explore easily. In the end, I bought a beautiful souvenir for my mom. It was convenient to carry home.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe an unforgettable trip you have taken. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you went and who you went with.", "What you did there.", "Explain why it was unforgettable."]
model_answer: Well, my most unforgettable trip was to Japan. Actually, I went there with my family two years ago. To be honest, we didn't plan a strict itinerary. While we were walking through Kyoto, we discovered a breathtaking old temple. Fortunately, our accommodation was very convenient and close to the subway. Also, I bought a traditional fan as a souvenir. To sum up, we loved to explore the city, even though we got lost once! We barely avoided the traffic congestion on the way to the airport.

1. While I (walk) __________, I (see) __________ a cat.

2. She (cook) __________ dinner when the phone (ring) __________.

3. They (play) __________ football when it (start) __________ to rain.
